description: Overall it's fine, but a few tweaks to the way the software operates will help it be a more seamless experience.
author: Anna C.
pros: Great for transparency across colleague's work and their obligation on certain tasks
cons: You can't click back without the page messing up. Sometimes colleague feedback is overwhelming and you can't really tell the order that it's in.

author: Eudoria S.
pros: I love the clear audit trail because it helps the team stay accountable and we can track where everything is at.
cons: I think the interface could be a little more user friendly and could be slightly more aesthetically pleasing especially when comparing the interface with other similar platforms.

description: I am a new user to the platform, and barely got used to the previous user interface before it changed. I think the new interface is slightly more intuitive but still has a learning curve. I don't like that you can't navigate to the feedback/approval page directly from the deliverable homepage - you have to go through the approvals button (i know i've tried that multiple times). I do like the project/deliverable layout. Would be nice if you could like projects.
author: Jeremy C.
pros: I like the automatic scheduling of approval level deadlines and the auto reminders to keep projects moving.
cons: The review process has some speedbumps - specifically markups done in acrobat pro are not visible in the pdf viewer, tracked changes on word doesn't work very well and comments don't show or say 'deleted'. Making iterative changes isn't very straightforward if you want to execute a change before the next level, you have to recall or submit the new version for approval, and then that gets confusing.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Admation is excellent for retrieving old work but terrible for it's job numbering system
description: It's got better but still takes a lot of steps to carry out a simple upload, it logs you out to quickly and and it doesn't save PDF's but rather jpegs of PDF's for markups so you cannot read fine detail like the previous job number on a piece of artwork.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I can see all the jobs I have worked on over several years.
cons: The job numbers should be alpha numeric, they number by itself is close to useless.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Managing tons of artworks and assets was a breeze with this system. Having an approval and audit system made it a lot easier in keeping track of which assets need revisions and which ones are approved.
cons: UX could be improved when it comes to the speed and responsiveness of the site. As projects keep adding more deliverables, it takes longer for the system to index and load. Messaging system could use some work as well as it's still a bit clunky (e.g. adding recipients, forwarding messages, etc.)

description: I am slowly getting used to the Admation software and finding it ok. Overall I find it very busy, there is too much information which is unnecessary which makes it hard to use and understand. Its not a user friendly or intuitive platform and could be designed to be much more appealing to the eye.
author: Kathryn M.
pros: Keeps a good record of all our partner sign offs.
cons: Its not easy to edit, use and approve items. It requires more customisation to suit the business use or purpose of the software. It should be a system that is so intuitive that you don't require training like Pageproof, which is used by our white-label partner. It would be really helpful if you had business best practice for use, because the process is much bigger than just the platform i.e. file types used etc. which could be communicated through the platform.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: It is usable however not intuitive enough for today's requirements. It might simply be a training issue though and i need more support to understand how i would perform searches effectively.
author: Dee T.
pros: There is a central source for documentation
cons: I am still unable to search for old approvals effectively. For instance, I wanted to find out what forms were updated for Blua last year and i didn't get the full suite.

description: I love it. First of all, it give me peace of mind that our enterprise compliance around signoff is met. secondly, the Admation is great for tracking progression of a job/ include reminders. Also, you can customise your dashboard to see all your projects and where all your current jobs are.
author: Laura S.
pros: It easy to use and it helps our team to streamline our approval workflow - it manage and track all of our reviews and approvals from one central/cloud based hub.
cons: It is a brilliant collaboration tool - very easy to access and manage.

review_title: Great platform for the whole marketing team to use
author: Michael S.
pros: It's a collaborative artwork management tool that keeps an audit history of changes, comments and approvals - so you always know where everything is at with each campaign.
cons: Can be a bit complicated to work through different sections and find a particular action point. But overall it's pretty intuitive.

description: AdMation helps keep a good record of our chain of approvals on graphic assets and makes it fairly simple for senior staff to review work.
author: Stephanie B.
pros: There are a lot of comprehensive features that allow team members to perform different tasks, depending on their job role.
cons: I don't find it particularly intuitive to use - but of a steep learning curve
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Helps us to gather our marketing artwork in one place and has cut down on our emails sourcing approvals & changes from large groups
author: Rebecca S.
pros: Great for tracking artwork versions & gathering approvals across a large team
cons: Annotations tools require further enhancement (visibility & late annotation additions) and deadline tools could be simpler and more visible for easy prioritisation.

author: Jennifer L.
pros: It is easy to use and understandEmail notifications when you tag someoneEasy to send reminders to peopleDeadline feature help people keep their work on track
cons: Some features still need work, e.g. marking up comments on videos before we submit for approvalWhen you submit a new revision for approval you need to re-select the approvers again which can sometimes mess up the flow

review_title: An update from the Simple platform
author: Jullie P.
pros: The search feature on Admation is more intuitive than the Simple platform. All the filters applied work and help narrow down the results shown.
cons: We can't send one document to multiple partners at the same time on the same level of approval.

description: Can take it or leave it.
author: Maria A.
pros: Hard to say , nothing really stands out as a wow feature for me.
cons: 1. I cannot download a word version of my document from the software! Marking up a pdf is so much more time consuming (and has less functionality) than marking up a word version and not everyone has the version of Adobe to allow them to edit a pdf. Often the reviewers are not necessarily those who actually have access to the software, therefore it is a waste of time for me to mark up someone else's changes within the software rather than uploading a marked up word version as an attachment to an email (which is what I've done lately) (ie when the Chairman often wants to make changes himself) 2. If I make changes to a document and then submit my changes, there is no functionality to amend or add further changes to the same document, prior to the team picking up the job. 3. The view functionality is not great, when you zoom in and make the text larger, you lose resolution very quickly. 4. One team member accused me of not including her on emails within the software, however I had done so. Not sure if there is an issue with the email within the software... 5. It can be confusing to navigate at times.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Good but still could be better
description: Good but not great, could definitely be more user friendly
author: Valentina S.
pros: Great for proofing particularly being in a smallish creative team
cons: Not intuitive and updates are buggy. Freezes and logs out on you multiple times a day

review_title: Hard to use, long process for approval
description: Slow, too many options and fields, is not set out in a user friendly manner for quick submissions. Does not advise of the companies requirements for submitting through admation and how those things should be submitted. Does not appear to be many people within the business that can provide support for using this, or at least in my area of the business.
author: David R.
pros: I like the idea of channelling work through the correct teams
cons: Does not tell you which teams each type of content must go through. Some of the options buttons are hard to tell which is selected, for example when selected for review by 'any' or 'all' reviewers. I also don't like how emails are not provided when content is approved, it means you could go weeks waiting as usually it takes that long for things to be approved, and then not realise its approved.

review_title: A powerful tool for tracking artwork submission and approvals.
description: I can see what my jobs are and importantly what their job numbers are so I can track the artwork down from the server. I can also find relevant material to a job assuming the client has remembered to upload anything.
author: Ben C.
pros: 1. You can see where all your current jobs are, what their job numbers are and if they have been reviewed and or approved. 2. The preview is fast. 3. Great for tracking progression of a job. 4. The Admation team bring out regular improvements to the software.
cons: 1. It involves too many steps to upload a single piece of artwork. 2. It generates a different job number for every element of a campaign, if you have 60 pieces of artwork you have 60 different job numbers for one campaign. Surely the description of the file dictates the difference without the need for seperate job numbers. 3. Overly complicated at times. 4. Markups to PDF's are very messy. Shame it doesn't use Acrobat to markup.

review_title: Slow servers result in delayed response, clunky systems
description: It's a frustrating system and is hard to work with. Wish there was more customisation of briefs and proof review/approval process.
author: Timothy B.
pros: Not a whole lot. The customer service our team is provided is good, but the system itself is generally cumbersome and frustrating to work with.
cons: Asset creation is clunky, there is no way to edit the way briefs populate, servers are constantly non responsive and slow.

review_title: So frustrating I usually just end up downloading file to view.
description: I use Admation to review/mark-up items other people have created and I find it a very frustrating tool. I usually end up downloading the whole file to see it properly even though I am only interested in 1 or 2 pages. Should be able to download one or 2 pages rather than whole document. The viewing pane is too small to read when item is full page. But then when you zoom in, the part of page you can see is too large and you cant see it in context. It should enlarge incrementally so you can just zoom a little bit if you need, or keep zooming the bigger you need it. The viewing pane should take up more of the page, and the other parts should take up less of the page. When you zoom in the viewing pane should get bigger again. You should be able to see multiple (at least 2) pages in the viewing pane at the same time. Also when you press the zoom button it should automatically enlarge rather than you then having to click on the viewing pane. I don't like that once you approve or decline you can not respond again. If it is still before dead-line time you should be able to go back in and mark-up again or change your initial mark-ups. I don't like that you cant format the comments/mark-ups you make. Should at least be able to make bold or change text size. When I add attachments markup comment nobody can see/access them. From the dashboard home page I can not tell if I have actually responded to the request. I have to open and see if I have. Thanks.
author: Tui K.
author_profession: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: I now love Admation as it is a program that both the creative team and I find helpful when trying to get projects across swiftly. At the beginning, I struggled with the use of the program; I couldn't find functions and commands, but I slowly started to get the hang of things. There is one thing that I constantly still struggle with. Creatives cannot see my message after I have approved artwork. I usually thank them (and sometimes ask them to high res the file I have approved). After several rounds of amends, it would be nice to have them see my message and next step to finalize the artwork. Thanks for the awesome program!
author: Romina T.
pros: the fact that my colleagues love the program as well. makes things easier.
cons: As explained before, creatives cannot see my message after artwork has been approved.

description: peace of mind that our enterprise complaince around signoff is met
author: Tom R.
pros: Admation ensures every of our creative that goes to market has been gisned off by relevant stakeholders (provided people remember to use it). Easy to approve work (as an approver)
cons: can become very frustrating when there are countelss rounds of signoff... the tool is used in our organisation as a complicated collaborative working tool that generates resentment at times when things are submitted again aand again for minor changes when a phone /meeting or email could get everyone on the same page. very annoying that you have to download word docs to mark them up and it can't be done in the software

author: Luke H.
pros: very user friendly and easy to master - a great tool when you have many teams needing input into artwork.
cons: would be great to have a feature where you could mock up a page to send back to the marketing team to save drawing this up on word and saving it as an attachment

review_title: Ease in use for integration of business
author: Surabhi S.
pros: This product is an important link between departments to communicate and showcase promos for catalogue.
cons: Improvement - its very slow to load at times, filter/searches view could be better.

description: It is a great tool when it works properly. It would be great to be able to bulk download artwork as currently we have to download individually which is very time consuming.
author: Sophie P.
pros: It keeps our artwork reviewing in one place.
cons: There are quite often bugs when using it and i have to refresh and start again when using it.

author: Haylee D.
pros: very easy to use the images that we see are very clear
cons: when you approve the page you can't get back into it or have it allocated again
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months
